zotero.abstractNote: Causal learning from observational data is a fundamental problem in machine learning. A common approach is to use structural causal models (SCMs) and to estimate the causal structure from data. However, in many real-world scenarios, the number of variables is large, and the number of samples is small, making the estimation of the causal structure difficult. In this paper, we propose a new approach to causal learning that combines interpolation and regularization. We show that this approach can be used to learn causal structures from observational data even in the high-dimensional setting. We provide theoretical guarantees for our approach and demonstrate its effectiveness on synthetic and real-world datasets.
